description: A state-sponsored threat actor used a custom anti-forensic tool to wipe the contents of an NTFS volume. A hardware recovery lab needs to know which physical clusters correspond to the deleted files in /home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd. Create a forensic case named 'MFT_Data_Run_Analysis' (Case: INV-MFT-008) and analyze the deleted files (excluding directories and NTFS metadata). Determine the residency of the primary unnamed $DATA attribute for each file. For non-resident files, extract the starting cluster and length of the first data run. Generate a tab-delimited report at /home/ga/Reports/data_run_map.tsv with the header: FILENAME\tINODE\tRESIDENCY\tSTARTING_CLUSTER\tCLUSTER_LENGTH. Use the base MFT Entry Number for INODE. Residency must be 'RESIDENT' or 'NON_RESIDENT' (use 'N/A' for cluster fields if resident). Save a summary to /home/ga/Reports/mft_summary.txt containing exactly: 'TOTAL_DELETED_FILES_ANALYZED: <N>', 'RESIDENT_FILES: <N>', 'NON_RESIDENT_FILES: <N>', and 'INVESTIGATOR_NOTES: Cluster mapping completed for hardware recovery lab.'
